result:
Done. Build is green.

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-lex/src/lib.rs:1947` — `str2num_stub` parsed hex integers with `i64::from_str_radix`, which rejects values that exceed `i64::MAX`. Lua 5.4's `l_str2int` (`reference/lua-5.4.7/src/lobject.c:285-288`) instead accumulates hex digits into a `lua_Unsigned` (u64) and wrap-casts to `lua_Integer`, so `0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF` becomes `-1`. The stub also lacked C99-style hex-float parsing (`0xF0.0`, `0x1.8p+1`), which the test uses on its next line.

**Fix:** Replaced the stub body to delegate to `lua_vm::object::str2num`, which already correctly implements `l_str2int` (u64 wrap), `lua_strx2number` (hex floats), and decimal/float fallbacks. The local `NumResult` enum still bridges to the lexer's expected return type. Updated the PORT STATUS trailer note.

**Verification:** `cargo build -q -p lua-cli` is clean (warnings only, no errors). Did not re-run the test program per the instructions; the mega-loop will re-test.
